title: A New Perspective on Predictive Motor Signaling.

assets.abstract: Adaptive behavior relies on complex neural processing in multiple interacting networks of both motor and sensory systems. One such interaction employs intrinsic neuronal signals, so-called 'corollary discharge' or 'efference copy', that may be used to predict the sensory consequences of a specific behavioral action, thereby enabling self-generated (reafferent) sensory information and extrinsic (exafferent) sensory inflow to be dissociated. Here, by using well-established examples, we seek to ide

phase_mapping.bridge_rationale: Nested agency — voluntary action, free will as constructed experience, intentional binding.
